typography.primary_font: System (San Francisco on iOS, Roboto on Android) or Inter

border_radius.button: rounded-full (9999px) or rounded-lg for industrial blockiness

shadows.card: shadow-sm elevation-2 (iOS/Android specific or NativeWind standard)

layouts.general: Mobile-first, single column. Content constrained to device width. Generous whitespace for readability outdoors.
layouts.dashboard: Vertical scroll list of jobs. Each job is a card component with title, status, and arrow indicator.
layouts.job_execution: Vertical stepper/list. Main task shown prominently. Yes/No buttons side-by-side or stacked based on text length. Nested subtasks appear indented with a left border line (timeline style) in #CBD5E1.
layouts.admin_panel: List views for CRUD operations. Simple form layouts with clear input fields and labels.
layouts.bottom_action: Sticky bottom container with safe-area padding for primary actions (like 'Ferdig' button).

components.name: Header
components.rules: Solid background #00407F, white logo/text. DO NOT use transparent backgrounds. Must include safe area insets.

components.name: Job Card
components.rules: White background, #CBD5E1 border 1px, 8px border radius. Title in bold, status indicator (e.g. In Progress, Not Started). Min height 72px.

components.name: Task Checkbox / Action
components.rules: Instead of standard checkboxes, use large Segmented Controls or twin buttons for Yes/No (Ja/Nei) for field workers wearing gloves. Ja = Green outline/fill, Nei = Red outline/fill.

components.name: Ferdig Button
components.rules: Primary CTA. Large, prominent block button. Background #00407F, white text. Min height 56px for thumb friendliness.

components.name: Indented Subtask Tree
components.rules: When 'Ja' or 'Nei' expands a subtask, render the subtask below the parent with a left margin (e.g., ml-4) and a connecting vertical line (border-l-2 border-slate-300) to visually group the workflow tree.

motion.transitions: Use React Native Reanimated or LayoutAnimation for expanding/collapsing subtasks. Ensure smooth but fast transitions (e.g. 200ms).
motion.interactions: Provide immediate visual feedback on Yes/No press (opacity change or background fill change).

accessibility.touch_targets: Minimum 48x48 logical pixels for all interactable elements (buttons, toggles, back arrows).
accessibility.contrast: Ensure text contrast meets WCAG AA standards. Use dark text on light backgrounds for outdoor visibility.
accessibility.test_ids: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='job-card', data-testid='task-ja-button').

image_urls.description: Geomatikk official wordmark logo (White/Dark Theme version, perfect for the #00407F header)
Woman engineer wearing safety gear, working on machine repair in an industrial setting. Use for login or empty dashboard.
A clipboard with a notepad attached to it. Use for empty job lists or no tasks state.

instructions_to_main_agent: Develop a React Native Expo application using expo-router.
Use NativeWind or StyleSheet for styling based on the provided design system.
Since this is an app for field workers, avoid tiny UI elements. Prioritize clarity, large typography, and big touch targets over dense information.
Implement the task tree logic so that picking Yes/No can dynamically push new tasks to the array/list below it, utilizing LayoutAnimation or Reanimated for smooth expansion.
Ensure outdoor readability: #00407F primary elements, strong black text for content, #CBD5E1 for structural dividers.
Create the PDF generation functionality simulating 'Ferdig' button action with standard expo-print or react-native-html-to-pdf libraries.
The UI is strictly in Norwegian ('Ferdig', 'Arbeidsflyt-kontroll', 'Ja', 'Nei').
Admin config should be accessible in-app, allowing creation of task JSON structures without coding.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: 1. Prioritize React Native best practices over web practices. Do not use web-only CSS properties like 'backdrop-blur' without native equivalents. 2. Implement robust test IDs (data-testid) on every single interactable element and key screen view. 3. Mobile design thrives on safe areas, keyboard avoiding views, and ScrollViews; ensure no content is hidden behind device notches or bottom bars. 4. Never use generic 'AI slop' placeholders; use the provided images and brand terminology.
